goal: Move the runtime implementation behind the intelligence_serving namespace while preserving the top-level chip_runtime module through a compatibility wrapper.
constraints: Preserve existing import paths for callers and tests
Do not change runtime hook semantics in this tranche
behavioral_changes: The real runtime implementation now lives under chip_labs.intelligence_serving.
The old top-level chip_runtime module now acts as a compatibility wrapper.
Serving-surface modules now import the namespace-local runtime implementation instead of reaching through the top-level wrapper.
verification_targets: PYTHONPATH=src python -c "from chip_labs.chip_runtime import ChipHandle, execute_hook, load_portfolio; from chip_labs.intelligence_serving.chip_runtime import ChipHandle as moved_ChipHandle; from chip_labs.intelligence_serving import load_portfolio as api_load_portfolio; import chip_labs.hooks, chip_labs.cli; print('serving-runtime-move-imports-ok')"
follow_up: With runtime moved, the serving namespace now owns the full serving implementation surface behind compatibility wrappers.
The next structural work should be dependency cleanup across surfaces rather than more same-pattern serving moves.
